message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "single amulet (plain, newcomer first contract)"
Current error: Expected premium 71, received 115
Passing tests so far: 2

Failing test detail:
```ts
const result = runScenario({
  customer: { yearsWithMHPCO: 0 },
  steps: [{ op: "quote", items: [{ type: "amulet", material: "silver", enchantment: 0, cursed: false }] }],
});
expect(result).toEqual({ results: [{ premium: 71 }] });
```

Passing tests:
- empty items → 5
- single plain sword (newcomer, first contract) → 115 (100 base + 10 first ins + 5 fee)

Compute for amulet: 60 base + 6 first ins = 66 + 5 fee = 71

Generalize minimally — use item type for base premium. Modifiers: +10% first insurance on policy + 5 fee. Don't add features for tests not in the list above. Don't refactor.

Working dir: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-57-53_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code needed to make it pass.
Now I'll implement minimally to support both sword (100 base) and amulet (60 base), with first-insurance 10% surcharge and 5 fee.

content.content: The file /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-57-53_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts has been updated successfully.

content.text: Floating point issue. Let me fix it using integer math (multiply by 11 then divide by 10, or compute base + base/10).

input.old_string:
const base = items.reduce((sum: number, item: any) => sum + (BASE_PREMIUMS[item.type] ?? 0), 0);
    premium = base * 1.1 + PROCESSING_FEE;
input.new_string:
const base = items.reduce((sum: number, item: any) => sum + (BASE_PREMIUMS[item.type] ?? 0), 0);
    premium = base + base / 10 + PROCESSING_FEE;

content.text:
All 3 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Added a `BASE_PREMIUMS` lookup map for item types (`sword: 100`, `amulet: 60`). When items exist, premium = sum of base premiums + 10% first-insurance surcharge + 5 fee. When empty, premium = 5 fee only.
- **Result**: All tests now pass (3 passing, 29 todo).
- **Approach**: Minimal generalization driven by the test: replaced the hardcoded `115` with a type-keyed base lookup containing exactly the two types tested so far (sword and amulet). Computed first-insurance surcharge as `base + base/10` (instead of `base * 1.1`) to avoid floating-point error. No features added for future tests (no loyalty, follow-up, cursed, enchantment, runes, blocks, or rounding logic).

Relevant file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-57-53_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
